title: FDA Compliance & Reporting Assessment
description: Assessment covering FDA regulations, variance applications, reporting requirements, and jurisdictional issues.

questions.question: Which regulation governs laser product performance in the United States?
questions.explanation: 21 CFR 1040 (Code of Federal Regulations, Title 21, Part 1040) sets federal performance standards for laser products sold in the US, enforced by FDA's CDRH.

questions.question: What must an entertainment laser operator obtain from the FDA before performing shows with Class IIIb/IV lasers?
questions.explanation: Operators must obtain a variance (deviation from the performance standard) from FDA to use Class IIIb/IV lasers for entertainment purposes where the beam may be directed at or near audiences.

questions.question: What does Laser Notice 50 (LN50) primarily address?
questions.explanation: Laser Notice 50 provides FDA guidance on the use of Class IIIb and IV lasers for audience scanning in entertainment applications, establishing exposure criteria for audience areas.

questions.question: Within how many days must manufacturers report laser product defects that could cause injury?
questions.explanation: Under 21 CFR 803, manufacturers must submit a mandatory report within 30 days of learning of a malfunction that could cause or contribute to serious injury if it were to recur.

questions.question: Which agency enforces 21 CFR 1040?
options: FDA's Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH)
questions.explanation: The FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH) enforces 21 CFR 1040 and administers the electronic product radiation control program.

questions.question: If a state has stricter laser regulations than federal FDA standards, which applies?
options: Federal standards always supersede state standards
State standards apply — states can be stricter than federal minimums
Operators can choose which standard to follow
Neither — the operator must petition for a compromise standard
questions.explanation: States can impose requirements stricter than federal minimums. Operators must comply with both federal standards AND any stricter state requirements that apply.

questions.question: A variance from FDA is granted to whom?
options: The specific operator for specific show types
The venue where shows are held
questions.explanation: FDA variances are granted to specific operators (not equipment or venues) for specific show types described in the application. Operators cannot transfer variances to others.

questions.question: What is FDA Form 3632 used for?
options: Reporting laser product introductions by manufacturers
questions.explanation: FDA Form 3632 (Report of Accession) is used by laser product manufacturers to report the introduction of new laser products to FDA as required under 21 CFR 1040.
